the bug**
When multiple simple inputs are created, some of them are not expanded by default. This problem was reported by @mpalmada. 

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 21.09 (usegalaxy.eu)

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Import this history: https://usegalaxy.eu/u/palmadamarc/w/orgone-assembly-workflow
2. Run it.

**Expected behavior**
Simple inputs used for workflow logic are not expanded. In that specific case,  assembly name, enable scaffolding with Flye and version should be expanded.

**Screenshots**
![Screenshot from 2022-02-09 17-44-50](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/26678117/153248290-89162d83-4fb0-4273-b72f-124009d33f9f.png)

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13341","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13341/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13341/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13341/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13341","id":1128801445,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5DSCSl","number":13341,"title":"Order of Jobs in Workflow "Setting the run" window","user":{"login":"mpalmada","id":78851165,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc4ODUxMTY1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/78851165?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mpalmada","html_url":"https://github.com/mpalmada","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mpalmada/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mpalmada/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mpalmada/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mpalmada/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mpalmada/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mpalmada/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mpalmada/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mpalmada/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mpalmada/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-02-09T17:02:58Z","updated_at":"2022-02-09T17:02:58Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hi,

**Description of the bug**
I am not sure if it is a bug but the order of the different jobs within a window is not in the order I put them in the workflow (from left to right).


**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 21.09 ([usegalaxy.eu](http://usegalaxy.eu/))

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Import this history: https://usegalaxy.eu/u/palmadamarc/w/orgone-assembly-workflow
2. Look at the order of the jobs within the workflow.
3. Click run buttom 
4. Look at the order of the jobs and datasets in the "setting the run" window

**Expected behavior**

I expect to have the jobs ordered from left to right, having in the top the job that is in the left of the workflow pipeline.

**Screenshots**
![galaxy_order_previous](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/78851165/153251529-39b4fd09-454c-43ff-ba7b-8b87844219c3.PNG)

**Description of the bug**
I am not sure if it is a bug but the order of the different jobs within a window is not in the order I put them in the workflow (from left to right).


**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 21.09 ([usegalaxy.eu](http://usegalaxy.eu/))

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Import this history: https://usegalaxy.eu/u/palmadamarc/w/orgone-assembly-workflow
2. Look at the order of the jobs within the workflow.
3. Click run buttom 
4. Look at the order of the jobs and datasets in the "setting the run" window

**Expected behavior**

I expect to have the jobs ordered from left to right, having in the top the job that is in the left of the workflow pipeline.

**Screenshots**
![galaxy_order_previous](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/78851165/153251529-39b4fd09-454c-43ff-ba7b-8b87844219c3.PNG)
![galaxy_order_setting](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/78851165/153251544-48dfcb07-85b5-4d6c-866e-a35cb59a5a57.PNG)

When multiple simple inputs are created, some of them are not expanded by default. This problem was reported by @mpalmada. 

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 21.09 (usegalaxy.eu)

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Import this history: https://usegalaxy.eu/u/palmadamarc/w/orgone-assembly-workflow
2. Run it.

**Expected behavior**
Simple inputs used for workflow logic are not expanded. In that specific case,  assembly name, enable scaffolding with Flye and version should be expanded.
